req.title: Biller registration (boundary variant)
req.statement: A customer shall register a biller only after a 24-hour hold; withdrawals to new billers shall be deferred. Boundary behaviour at exact min/max thresholds must be tested.

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-048 governs biller registration with a mandatory 24-hour hold and deferred withdrawals. Key test dimensions are: (1) EP — a valid new biller lands in 'hold' state (positive path); (2) NEG — a duplicate biller registration returns HTTP 409 (error pathway); (3) BVA — the exact 24-hour boundary distinguishes 'hold' from 'active', so we test at t=24h-1s (still hold) and t=24h (eligible for active). An additional STC test covers the state machine arc from 'hold' to 'active' once the hold expires. Together these four cases give positive, negative, and two boundary variants, satisfying the minimum three required and providing full traceability to the requirement statement.

tests.name: test_register_new_biller_enters_hold_state
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with a valid JWT
Biller with the generated account number does not already exist for this customer
actions: Generate a unique biller payload via <unique_biller_payload>
POST /billers with the payload and Authorization header
expected: Response body contains a hold_until timestamp approximately 24 hours from now
trace: R-FIN-048: 'A customer shall register a biller only after a 24-hour hold'
tests.executable:
def test_register_new_biller_enters_hold_state(auth_headers, api_base_url, unique_biller_payload):
    import requests, datetime
    payload = unique_biller_payload()
    resp = requests.post(f"{api_base_url}/billers", json=payload, headers=auth_headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "hold"
    hold_until = datetime.datetime.fromisoformat(body["hold_until"])
    now = datetime.datetime.now(datetime.timezone.utc)
    delta = hold_until - now
    assert datetime.timedelta(hours=23, minutes=59) <= delta <= datetime.timedelta(hours=24, minutes=1)
    # Cleanup: remove biller so test is idempotent
    biller_id = body["id"]
    requests.delete(f"{api_base_url}/billers/{biller_id}", headers=auth_headers)

tests.name: test_duplicate_biller_registration_returns_409
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with a valid JWT
A biller with the target account number has already been registered for this customer
actions: Register a biller via <register_biller_fixture> to ensure it exists
Immediately POST /billers again with the identical payload
expected: Response body contains an error code indicating duplicate biller
No second biller record is created
tests.executable:
def test_duplicate_biller_registration_returns_409(auth_headers, api_base_url, register_biller_fixture):
    import requests
    payload = register_biller_fixture()  # creates biller and returns its payload
    # Attempt duplicate registration
    resp = requests.post(f"{api_base_url}/billers", json=payload, headers=auth_headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 409
    body = resp.json()
    assert "duplicate" in body.get("error", "").lower() or body.get("code") == "BILLER_DUPLICATE"

tests.name: test_biller_still_on_hold_at_24h_minus_1_second
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with a valid JWT
A biller was registered exactly 24 hours minus 1 second ago (simulated via <time_travel_fixture>)
actions: Register a biller and set its created_at to now - (24h - 1s) via <time_travel_fixture>
expected: Withdrawals to this biller are still deferred
trace: R-FIN-048: boundary behaviour at exact min/max thresholds
R-FIN-048: '24-hour hold' — one second before boundary must remain in hold
tests.executable:
def test_biller_still_on_hold_at_24h_minus_1_second(auth_headers, api_base_url, time_travel_fixture, unique_biller_payload):
    import requests, datetime
    payload = unique_biller_payload()
    create_resp = requests.post(f"{api_base_url}/billers", json=payload, headers=auth_headers)
    assert create_resp.status_code == 201
    biller_id = create_resp.json()["id"]
    # Move clock to 24h - 1s after registration
    offset = datetime.timedelta(hours=24) - datetime.timedelta(seconds=1)
    time_travel_fixture(offset)
    get_resp = requests.get(f"{api_base_url}/billers/{biller_id}", headers=auth_headers)
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    assert get_resp.json()["status"] == "hold"
    # Cleanup
    time_travel_fixture(datetime.timedelta(0))
    requests.delete(f"{api_base_url}/billers/{biller_id}", headers=auth_headers)

tests.name: test_biller_transitions_to_active_at_exact_24h_boundary
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with a valid JWT
A biller was registered and its created_at is set to exactly 24 hours ago via <time_travel_fixture>
actions: Register a biller and advance time by exactly 24 hours via <time_travel_fixture>
Optionally attempt a withdrawal to confirm it is no longer deferred
expected: Withdrawal is no longer deferred at the exact 24-hour mark
trace: R-FIN-048: boundary behaviour at exact min/max thresholds
R-FIN-048: state transition from 'hold' to 'active' at t == 24h
tests.executable:
def test_biller_transitions_to_active_at_exact_24h_boundary(auth_headers, api_base_url, time_travel_fixture, unique_biller_payload):
    import requests, datetime
    payload = unique_biller_payload()
    create_resp = requests.post(f"{api_base_url}/billers", json=payload, headers=auth_headers)
    assert create_resp.status_code == 201
    biller_id = create_resp.json()["id"]
    # Advance clock to exactly 24 hours after registration
    time_travel_fixture(datetime.timedelta(hours=24))
    get_resp = requests.get(f"{api_base_url}/billers/{biller_id}", headers=auth_headers)
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    assert get_resp.json()["status"] == "active"
    # Cleanup
    time_travel_fixture(datetime.timedelta(0))
    requests.delete(f"{api_base_url}/billers/{biller_id}", headers=auth_headers)
